name: Fainting
summary: Brief loss of consciousness from reduced blood flow to the brain. Usually recovers within a minute.

callEmergency.text: Call 999 if they don't come round within a minute or two, or if they faint repeatedly.

steps: Lay them on the floor — don't sit them on a chair (they could fall off).
Raise their legs above heart level to return blood to vital organs.
Plenty of fresh air, loosen tight clothing.
Stay with them until they feel well enough to sit up.

warnings.text: If someone faints repeatedly, they should see a doctor to rule out other causes.
